name: Apple Pancakes with Cider Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the biscuit mix and cinnamon.
2. In another bowl, whisk together the egg and milk until smooth.
3. Pour the egg mixture into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined.
4. Stir in the shredded apple.
5. Heat a lightly greased griddle or frying pan over medium heat.
6. For each pancake, scoop about 1/4 cup of batter onto the griddle.
7. Cook until bubbles form on the surface and the edges are set, about 2-3 minutes.
8. Flip the pancakes and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes on the other side.
9. Serve hot with Cider Sauce spooned over the top.
10. To make the sauce, in a heavy saucepan combine the sugar, cornstarch, cinnamon, and nutmeg.
11. Gradually stir in the apple cider and lemon juice until smooth.
12. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture comes to a boil.
13. Continue cooking for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
14. Remove from heat and stir in the butter or margarine.
15. Serve immediately. Makes about 13 4-inch pancakes (about half that if you make them larger).
